title: Question: Create a new instance of a Verilog module during execution
body:
---


Author Name: **Weber S**
Original Redmine Message: 1442 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi,

How can I create a copy of a given Verilog module (Vcount in this example) dynamically during the simulation? The obvious approach haven't worked because the copy constructor is declared as private:

int main() {
  Vcount *top = new Vcount();
  Vcount *top_new;
  ...
  top_new = new Vcount(*top);
...
}

./Vcount.h: In function ‘int main()’:
./Vcount.h:56: error: ‘Vcount::Vcount(const Vcount&)’ is private
../top.cpp:24: error: within this context

Vcount.h:    Vcount& operator= (const Vcount&); ///< Copying not allowed

Is there any reason for not implementing a deep copy? How could I create a new instance of Vcount, replicating all its internal state?

Tks in advance. And congrats for this great tool.
